The mechanic works the same way. Orange was placed on the first gray dot in the red box. The orange square in the bottom palette is now gray (used up).
Now I need to figure out the correct order. Top row: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M (7 colors). I placed orange first which matches.

- Red box: col 28, col 40 (col 34 is charcoal - might be a skip or pre-filled)
- Green box: col 22, 28, 34, 40
Total remaining: 6 gray dots. Remaining colors: p, R, b, N, Y, M (6 colors). 
The charcoal dot might represent a "skip" position. Let me continue filling in order:
- Red box col 28: purple (p) - 2nd top color
- Red box col 40: red (R) - 3rd top color (skip charcoal at col 34)
- Green box col 22: blue (b) - 4th top color
- Green box col 28: light green (N) - 5th top color
- Green box col 34: yellow (Y) - 6th top color
- Green box col 40: magenta (M) - 7th top color
Wait, but the charcoal dot is at col 34 in the red box. If I skip it, the red box would have O, p, _, R which doesn't match the top row O, p, R, b.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the charcoal dot needs to be filled too, making it 8 slots for 7 colors. Or maybe the charcoal dot represents one of the 7 colors already placed.
Actually, looking more carefully, maybe the charcoal dot is the "red" color (R) since the box is red-bordered and the 3rd top color is red. The charcoal might be how red appears when placed in a dot.
Let me try: fill remaining gray dots in left-to-right, top-to-bottom order with remaining top colors:
- Red box col 28: p (purple)
- Red box col 40: b (blue) -- skip charcoal which is R
- Green box col 22: N (light green)
- Green box col 28: Y (yellow)
- Green box col 34: M (magenta)
- Green box col 40: R (red) -- wait, but red is the 3rd color...
Hmm, let me think again. The top row is: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
The dots in reading order (left-to-right, top-to-bottom), skipping the charcoal:
1. Red box col 22: O ✓ (already placed)
2. Red box col 28: p
3. Red box col 40: R (skip charcoal at 34)
4. Green box col 22: b
5. Green box col 28: N
6. Green box col 34: Y
7. Green box col 40: M
That gives 7 colors for 7 gray dots. Let me try this.
Bottom palette remaining: R(col 10), p(col 17), N(col 24), M(col 38), b(col 45), Y(col 52)
